scenario_name: Scenario 14 — Anti-Pattern Detection in Existing Architecture
justification: All 6 pass criteria met: (1) All 5 anti-patterns identified by name — shared database, synchronous fan-in, god endpoint, REST-as-job-trigger, direct cross-service DB reads. (2) Prioritized remediation plan across 4 phases over 3-4 quarters. (3) Shared database and direct DB reads identified as highest risk in Phase 3 with immediate mitigations in Phase 1. (4) REST-based jobs explicitly called out as fragile due to HTTP timeouts, re-entrancy, and unauthorized access. (5) Trade-offs for shared database fix thoroughly covered including migration complexity, eventual consistency, and dual-write strategy. (6) Incremental Strangler Fig approach recommended, with explicit warnings against big-bang rewrite.

observations.text: All 5 anti-patterns identified with precise technical explanations of why each is harmful
Remediation clearly prioritized: Phase 1 immediate risk mitigation, Phase 2 Notification decoupling, Phase 3 database sovereignty, Phase 4 API cleanup
Shared database and direct DB reads correctly identified as highest risk with critical severity ratings
REST-based cron jobs identified as fragile with specific risks: unauthorized triggering, health check interference, lack of idempotency
Comprehensive trade-offs section for each remediation decision, including alternatives considered
Explicit anti-big-bang guidance in pitfalls_to_avoid section
Bonus: identified PCI compliance risk with Customer /everything endpoint returning payment methods — not in rubric but shows depth
Named appropriate architectural patterns: Strangler Fig, Event-Carried State Transfer, Circuit Breaker, Anti-Corruption Layer

Phase ordering places Notification decoupling (Phase 2) before database split (Phase 3), while rubric expects shared DB and direct DB reads as highest priority — though Phase 1 mitigates the immediate DB risks with compatibility tests and the rationale for async-first is defensible
Confidence areas and assumptions are well-calibrated, acknowledging unknowns like FK relationships and consumer counts

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"This system exhibits five distinct architectural anti-patterns accumulated over 3 years of organic growth. The most critical issues are the shared database between Order and Courier services (destroying service autonomy), the Reporting Service's direct database reads (creating invisible coupling), and the synchronous Notification Service fan-in (creating a single point of failure for 5 services). The god endpoint and HTTP-based cron jobs are lower severity but still degrade
